name: Health Data
description: Health status, medical conditions, disability, and health-related behavioral data. Includes both declared health information and health conditions inferred from behavioral signals.

regulatory_basis.name: GDPR Article 9(1)
regulatory_basis.summary: Health data is a special category of personal data. Processing requires explicit consent or another Article 9(2) legal basis.

regulatory_basis.name: US ADA
regulatory_basis.summary: Disability status is a protected class. Advertising targeting or exclusion based on disability is restricted in employment, housing, and public accommodations.

regulatory_basis.name: US HIPAA
regulatory_basis.summary: Health information used in advertising must comply with HIPAA when sourced from covered entities. Increasingly applied to inferred health data.

includes: Inferred health conditions from behavioral signals (e.g., visiting diabetes websites)
Fitness and wellness data when linked to health conditions
excludes: General fitness interests (e.g., 'gym member') without health condition inference
Dietary preferences (e.g., 'vegan') unless linked to medical necessity
Age — covered separately by age-related regulations, not health data
guidance: The boundary between general wellness interest and health data inference is the key edge case. A signal for 'yoga enthusiasts' is not health data; a signal for 'chronic pain management seekers' is. When in doubt, data providers should declare health_data and let governance agents evaluate.
